column8: action_or_policy
Preview alias retires; move to claude-mythos-5 before the cutoff.
Retired; Anthropic recommends claude-sonnet-4-6 and claude-opus-4-8 respectively.
Retired; Anthropic recommends claude-sonnet-4-6.
Retired; Anthropic recommends claude-haiku-4-5-20251001.
Retired; Cohere recommends rerank-v3.5.
Deprecated April 30, 2026 in Mistral's legacy table; Mistral lists Mistral Small 4 as the alternative for both models.
Deprecated May 22, 2026 in Mistral's legacy table; Mistral lists Mistral Medium 3.5 for devstral-2512 and magistral-medium-2509, and Ministral 3 8B for open-mistral-nemo-2407.
Deprecated May 22, 2026 in Mistral's legacy table; Mistral lists Mistral Medium 3.5 as the alternative.
Discontinued per the official model list; migrate to kimi-k2.6.
The official model overview lists this deprecation date and says deprecated models route automatically; it does not name a replacement for this series.
The provider scheduled shutdown for this date with requests automatically routed to GLM-4.7-Flash; the source page still uses future-tense wording.
A June 2026 notice set this removal date for all three models; for the two TTS v1 models, it superseded the earlier 2025-12-15 date. Migrate to eleven_multilingual_v2 or the current Scribe models.

column4: Legacy model aliases deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner
column8: Aliases currently map to deepseek-v4-flash non-thinking and thinking modes; move to explicit v4 model IDs before the cutoff.
Retirement; recommended replacement is claude-opus-4-8.

column4: July and August 2026 model deprecations (free and developer tiers)
column8: Applies to free and developer tiers only; committed-spend enterprise contracts are exempt.
Retirement date was set at release; Labs models also carry a training-data exception.
Removal from the API; migrate to current GPT-5.5/5.4 model IDs.
Earliest possible shutdown date per the deprecations table.
Shut down. Migrate image workloads to current model IDs.
Discontinued and no longer maintained; kimi-latest was discontinued 2026-01-28.

column4: grok-4-1-fast, grok-4-fast, grok-4-0709, grok-code-fast-1, grok-3, grok-imagine-image-pro
column8: Retired; requests to retired slugs auto-redirect to grok-4.3 at grok-4.3 pricing. Pin current model IDs to avoid a silent swap.
Retired; recommended replacements include embed-v4.0 and current Command models.
Shut down; migrate to gemini-3.1-pro-preview or a stable model.
The consumer Sora product was discontinued; the Sora API remained documented and available past this date, so product and API lifecycles must be tracked separately.
Scheduled discontinuation of the Sora API; migrate video-generation workloads before the cutoff. Rights, retention, and provenance terms for this surface are recorded in the media rights registry.
Published shutdown date for the Veo 2 and Veo 3 video model IDs; the pricing page still presented the models after the date passed, so pin veo-3.1 IDs and automate alias checks.
Deprecated and shut down; direct evidence that multimodal understanding models retire on short cycles, not only generation models.
Deprecated; update to Marengo 2.7 and API v1.3. The Generate API rename and the Marengo 2.7 sunset are separate dated events tracked below.
Compatibility window for the old Generate API method names ended; applications must call the renamed Analyze API methods.
Sunset: indexing, search, and embedding retrieval on 2.7 stopped working. Marengo 2.7 embeddings are not compatible with 3.0, so migration requires reindexing and regenerating all embeddings.
Official sunset of Gen-4 Aleph; Runway recommends upgrading to Aleph 2.0 (aleph2).
Published shutdown date for the Imagen 4 image model IDs; Google recommends migrating to Gemini 2.5 Flash Image.
Maintains a legacy-models table with per-model deprecation dates, retirement dates, and recommended alternatives; retirements land in dated waves.

Announces changes in a dated changelog; stable aliases are re-pointed to new models in place, temporary comparison endpoints carry explicit expiry timestamps, and alias retirements are announced with a cutoff date.
Marks discontinued models with dates and a migration target on the official model list; discontinued slugs can remain visible on the page after their date.
Publishes an upcoming-deprecation table with per-model dates and says deprecated model codes automatically route to newer models; a past date alone does not independently confirm shutdown behavior.

Lists deprecated models and removal dates on the models page; the breaking-changes policy promises outreach before endpoint removal but states no fixed minimum notice period, and removal dates have moved.

Lifecycle disclosure is model-specific and inconsistent: gen4_aleph carries a dated sunset in the catalog, other models have carried deprecated badges without dates, and gen3a_turbo was removed from the catalog entirely. A deprecated badge alone does not establish a migration window.

Publishes deprecations in release notes with compatibility deadlines; major model upgrades can require regenerated, incompatible embeddings, so lifecycle cost includes reindexing rather than only a model-string change.
Current Ray API pages coexist with legacy model documentation and a migration guide; documentation drift can leave integrations pinned to superseded model IDs after the commercial product moves on.
Deprecations are announced on a dated page with removal dates; preview models can retire on much shorter notice.
Deprecations are dated with retirement dates and recommended replacements; partner platforms (Bedrock, Vertex) set their own retirement schedules.
Stable models get published earliest-shutdown dates; preview models can be deprecated with at least two weeks notice; -latest aliases hot-swap with two weeks notice.
Retired model slugs automatically redirect to the current model rather than erroring; behavior and pricing change silently unless IDs are pinned.
Deprecated means closed to new customers; existing users (any use within 90 days of the announcement) keep access until the retirement date.
Production models get email notice, a transition period, and a recommended replacement; deprecation exposure depends on contract tier, and preview models can be discontinued at short notice.
Three-day notice for compatible same-lineage redirects, two-week timelines for material changes, and under 24 hours for preview models after 30 days.
At least two weeks advance notice before removing any serverless model.
